title: NAC Policy Compliance Trending
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" earliest=-30d
| eval outcome=case(match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)permit|access.accept"),"permit", match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)deny|reject"),"deny", true(),"other")
| timechart span=1d count by outcome
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.action Authentication.vendor_action span=1d
| sort -count
description: Daily percentage of authentications that receive ‚Äúpermit‚Äù vs ‚Äúdeny‚Äù vs ‚Äúredirect‚Äù per authorization policy ‚Äî trending for policy drift and rollout validation.
value: Daily percentage of authentications that receive ‚Äúpermit‚Äù vs ‚Äúdeny‚Äù vs ‚Äúredirect‚Äù per authorization policy ‚Äî trending for policy drift and rollout validation.
grandmaExplanation: Daily percentage of authentications that receive ‚Äúpermit‚Äù vs ‚Äúdeny‚Äù vs ‚Äúredirect‚Äù per authorization policy ‚Äî trending for policy drift and rollout validation ‚Äî so unsafe traffic and policy bypasses are caught quickly.
implementation: Normalize `authorization_result` from your ISE field set. Alert when `deny` share increases >2√ó baseline week-over-week.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk_TA_cisco-ise.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:ise:auth`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Normalize `authorization_result` from your ISE field set. Alert when `deny` share increases >2√ó baseline week-over-week.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" earliest=-30d
| eval outcome=case(match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)permit|access.accept"),"permit", match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)deny|reject"),"deny", true(),"other")
| timechart span=1d count by outcome
```

Understanding this SPL

**NAC Policy Compliance Trending** ‚Äî Daily percentage of authentications that receive ‚Äúpermit‚Äù vs ‚Äúdeny‚Äù vs ‚Äúredirect‚Äù per authorization policy ‚Äî trending for policy drift and rollout validation.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ise:auth`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_cisco-ise. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:auth. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **outcome** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by outcome** ‚Äî ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.action Authentication.vendor_action span=1d
| sort -count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**NAC Policy Compliance Trending** ‚Äî Daily percentage of authentications that receive ‚Äúpermit‚Äù vs ‚Äúdeny‚Äù vs ‚Äúredirect‚Äù per authorization policy ‚Äî trending for policy drift and rollout validation.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ise:auth`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_cisco-ise. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked area (outcomes over time), Line chart (deny rate), Single value (deny % today).
visualization: Stacked area (outcomes over time), Line chart (deny rate), Single value (deny % today).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
